label_validity.interpretation: MODERATE — cascade disagrees with formula labels on 34% of test samples
label_validity.note: Baseline = formula labels (synthetic). Cascade = learned boundaries. Disagreement = potential label errors.

key_findings: Trivial tier: 99.46% agreement — formula labels are highly reliable for simple prompts
Light tier: 69.32% — ~30% of light prompts may have incorrect formula labels
Moderate tier: 28.09% — HIGH disagreement, formula labels unreliable
Heavy tier: 34.63% — HIGH disagreement, formula labels unreliable
Intensive tier: 8.32% — EXTREME disagreement, formula labels nearly meaningless
Extreme tier: 38.33% but only 60 samples — too few to judge
recommendations: Use formula labels as-is for trivial tier only (99.46% reliable)
Run LLM-as-judge on light/moderate/heavy prompts to establish ground truth
Collect ~500 stratified samples per tier for LLM labeling
Retrain cascade on LLM-validated labels to replace formula-based labels
For production routing, use cascade with confidence scoring — low-confidence predictions flag for review
